The branch adds positional cron add/create job names, first-class `--webhook` delivery for cron add/create/edit, CLI regression tests, and cron documentation examples.

PR surface: Source +52, Tests +96, Docs +30. Total +178 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection and mocked CLI tests cover argument-to-RPC payload mapping, but they do not prove a real Gateway/webhook run.

target.title: feat: improve cron create delivery ergonomics
target.body:
## Summary

- Add positional job names for `openclaw cron add/create` while keeping `--name` supported.
- Add first-class `--webhook <url>` delivery on `cron add/create` and `cron edit`, with conflicts against chat delivery flags.
- Update cron CLI and scheduled-task docs with the shorter create path and webhook examples.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm check:changed` on Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksrk5v484pz4s0gmtebmjhgx` / run `26611114584`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: cron creation can use a positional name and webhook delivery without hand-editing job JSON or relying on undocumented delivery fields.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout plus delegated Blacksmith Testbox changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm check:docs`; `git diff --check`; autoreview branch diff.

Evidence after fix: cron CLI regression tests assert positional names, webhook add/create payloads, webhook edit payloads, and conflict errors for mixed webhook/chat delivery.

Observed result after fix: all targeted tests and changed gates passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: live Gateway cron execution against an external webhook endpoint.
target.author: steipete
